subheadline: Give humans and agents complete context to understand, oversee, and evolve the world's largest, most complex codebases.
value_proposition: Sourcegraph indexes your entire codebase to give AI agents and engineers full context, preventing missed changes, inconsistency, and risk at scale.
target_audience: Enterprise engineering teams managing large, complex codebases who are adopting AI coding agents

testimonials: Minions are connected to MCP… this is how they gather context: internal docs, ticket details, build statuses, and code intelligence via Sourcegraph search. — Alistair Gray, Stripe

pricing.price_anchoring: Value framed around agent efficiency gains (30% cost reduction, 38% speed increase) and risk reduction at enterprise scale; pricing not shown, demo required

features: Deep Search: agentic natural language AI search with grounded answers and citations
MCP Server: SCIP-powered code graph knowledge for AI agents across all repositories
Code Search: exact, deterministic, exhaustive search across the entire codebase
Code Insights: track migrations, adoption, and risk metrics over time
Batch Changes: large-scale, cross-repository automated changes and agentic migrations
differentiators: SCIP-powered code intelligence giving agents complete cross-repository context vs. fragment-level context from other tools
Indexes all repositories across entire codebase, not just open files
Trusted by enterprise for over a decade for deterministic search
Zero data retention — LLM inference never stored or shared with third parties
Built to handle the world's largest monorepos and multi-repo architectures
trust_signals: Dedicated Account Managers and Support Engineers
Named customer: Stripe with named employee testimonial

design_patterns.layout: Full-width sections with alternating content blocks; hero with large statement text, problem/solution narrative flow, interactive code comparison demo, feature carousels, and enterprise trust section at bottom
design_patterns.color_scheme: Dark background with white and light gray text; accent colors for code highlights; minimal use of brand color
design_patterns.typography: Large, bold display type for headlines; clean sans-serif body; monospace for code snippets
design_patterns.visual_style: Technical and minimal with bold typographic statements; developer-native aesthetic
notable_patterns: Side-by-side agent comparison demo (with vs. without Sourcegraph)
Scrolling ticker for code monitoring alerts
Quote callout with customer name and title

emotional_tone: confident and urgent — frames the problem as an incoming 'tidal wave' and positions Sourcegraph as control and survival
key_objections_addressed: Agents already have access to code — rebutted by showing agents only see fragments without Sourcegraph
Security and compliance concerns — addressed with SOC2, ISO27001, zero data retention
Scalability concerns — addressed with explicit claim of handling world's largest monorepos
Agent reliability — addressed with before/after demo showing missed layers without full context
missing_or_weak: No visible pricing page content or even tier names — high friction for self-serve evaluation
Only one named customer (Stripe) with a logo; '200+ enterprise teams' claim lacks supporting logos
No free trial or self-serve signup path — every CTA requires sales contact
No video or live demo embed in hero — the agent comparison is text-based, not visual
No named competitor comparisons or explicit 'vs.' positioning despite clear differentiation opportunity
CodeScaleBench report mentioned in a modal/overlay but not integrated into the main narrative
Cody (AI coding assistant) listed in nav but not featured or explained on the page
standout_elements: The side-by-side agent comparison (with vs. without Sourcegraph) is highly concrete and specific — names exact missed files like auth middleware, audit logging, frontend routes
Stripe testimonial names a specific person, role, and use case — not a generic quote
Problem framing ('tidal wave of code') creates urgency without being alarmist
CodeScaleBench benchmark (30% cost, 38% speed, 2-3x retrieval) gives quantified ROI that most competitors lack
Clear three-part framework (Understand. Oversee. Evolve.) gives the platform coherent narrative structure
